title: Decaloba biflora
intro: Decaloba biflora, also known as Wild Poinciana, is a plant species in the family Passifloraceae, native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may help to reduce pain, fever, and swelling. The bioactive constituents of Decaloba biflora include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Decaloba biflora is commonly used in herbal preparations, such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, often used to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions.
taxonomy: Decaloba biflora is a species of flowering plant in the family Passifloraceae. It is a member of the genus Decaloba, which is a group of plants that are commonly known as passion flowers. The Passifloraceae family is a large and diverse group of plants, with over 550 species found across the world. Decaloba biflora is native to the tropical regions of Central and South America.
